steps.title: Find angle T using the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The three angles of triangle $RST$ sum to $180°$. Setting up the equation:

$$50 + x + (4x - 5) = 180$$

Combining like terms:

$$45 + 5x = 180$$

Solving for $x$:

$$5x = 135$$
$$x = 27$$

Now we can find angle $T$:

$$\text{Angle } T = 4x - 5 = 4(27) - 5 = 108 - 5 = 103°$$

steps.title: Apply corresponding angles with parallel lines
steps.content: Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, with $\overline{ST}$ as a transversal cutting the two parallel lines, angle $SKL$ and angle $STR$ are corresponding angles. When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are equal. Therefore, angle $SKL$ = angle $T$ = 103 degrees.
